title: Nursery Rhyme. DXLVI. Natural History.
content:
[The Proverb of Barnaby Bright is given by Ray and Brand as referring to St. Barnabas.]

        Barnaby Bright he was a sharp cur,
        He always would bark if a mouse did but stir;
        But now he's grown old, and can no longer bark,
        He's condemn'd by the parson to be hanged by the clerk.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. DXLVI. Natural History.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
